---
title: "Terminology"
subtitle: "Controlled language used across the explorer"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
---

<div class="page-intro">
This page documents the controlled terminology used for specimen type, anatomical site, disease labels, omics layers, access labels, and reuse-readiness labels. The goal is to make filtering predictable while preserving uncertainty when source metadata are incomplete.
</div>

## Controlled Vocabulary

<div id="terminology-vocabulary"></div>

---

## Core Conventions

- Use <strong>Specimen Type</strong> for the physical biospecimen, such as colonic biopsies, stool samples, PBMC, serum, or resection tissue.
- Use <strong>Anatomical Site</strong> for location, such as terminal ileum, colon, rectum, blood, or stool.
- Use <strong>Crohn disease</strong> and <strong>ulcerative colitis</strong> rather than abbreviations in curated fields.
- Use <strong>stool samples</strong> for specimen type and <strong>stool</strong> for anatomical site.
- Preserve <strong>Unclear</strong> when the source does not support a confident label.

<div class="disclaimer">
<strong>Conservative annotation.</strong> Terminology harmonization should not introduce new clinical claims. Ambiguous source labels remain manual-review targets.
</div>
